repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Methodist Episcopal Church of Bath records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Methodist Episcopal Church of Bath (Bath, N.Y.)

did.abstract: One volume of preprinted register containing lists of baptisms, marriages, probationers, members and classes.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: In 1814 Bath, which had 10 Methodists, was included on the Newtown (now Elmira) circuit in the Genesee Conference. In 1816 an auxiliary of the American Bible Society was organized by Methodist and Presbyterian women. By 1819 Bath was probably on the Dansville circuit. Religious services and Bible school were held in the courthouse until 1819 when it was forbidden by the supervisors. Thereafter, until the completion of the church in 1826, the members met in each other's homes. The church building was used by many other churches at various times. In 1846 it was remodeled. In 1866 the church changed its name to Centenary Methodist Episcopal Church when it built a new church.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: This register lists baptisms, marriages, probationers, members and classes (1858-1870) with the usual information in a printed format. No doubt other volumes existed which are not extant.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Methodist Episcopal Church of Bath records, #6092. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Steuben County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Methodist church buildings.
Methodist Episcopal Church -- New York (State) -- Bath.

did.unittitle: Record of the Methodist Episcopal Church Bath, Register,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists date of the baptism, name of the baptized person and, if an infant, the name of its parents or guardians, the date of birth of baptized person, the residence of the party baptized, the name of the officiating minister and the names of witnesses. 1858-1870. (4 double pages).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists date, name of parties, residence(s), place of marriage officiating minister and witnesses. 1857-1869. (16 double pages).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Double exposure. Lists date, name, condition (married, single, widowed), residence, class leader and remarks. 1857-1870. (11 double pages).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes name, residence, date and mode of reception, and the name of the class leader. Any changes are indicated as "remarks." 1853-1870. (28 double pages).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists names, leaders and when and where meets for up to 10 classes, some revised several times. (34 pages).
